outcome_text: Contingency processes are in place for third-party AI systems or data.
scoring_text: Skill in developing contingency plans for third-party AI dependencies, including backup strategies for third-party system failures, data continuity planning, alternative sourcing procedures, and business continuity measures for AI supply chain disruptions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in developing contingency plans for third-party AI dependencies, including backup strategies for third-party system failures, data continuity planning, alternative sourcing procedures, and business continuity measures for AI supply chain disruptions.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in negotiating strategic vendor partnerships for data and AI platforms, including commercial terms, roadmap alignment, dependency management, and exit strategy planning.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-6.2 (GOVERN) superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-013 is the strongest match (STS: 0.620, cosine: 0.642). Secondary: AG-A-003 (AG, cosine: 0.608), AG-T-005 (AG, cosine: 0.604).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.620). Shared vocabulary: [ai, data, planning, skill]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [alternative, backup, business, chain, contingency, continuity] vs WIDAI terms [alignment, commercial, dependency, exit, management, negotiating] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.893 (P=0.885, R=0.902). Balanced P/R — the 31-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (94.2%). Semantic tension between "Skill in developing contingency plans for third-party AI dependencies, including" and "Skill in negotiating strategic vendor partnerships for data and AI platforms, in". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.642 ≈ STS 0.620 (gap 0.022). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, data, planning, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, data, planning, skill]. NIST AI RMF-only: [alternative, backup, business, chain, contingency, continuity]. WIDAI-only: [alignment, commercial, dependency, exit, management, negotiating].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 94.2% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.620 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
